api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/a.purpose: Async (asyncio) entry point of the generated OpenMeter Python SDK — exposes OpenMeterClient built on AsyncPipelineClient with one attribute per resource group. All async I/O lives here; request construction is delegated entirely to the sync operations layer in the parent package. Generated code — customize only via _patch.py.

patterns.name: AsyncPipelineClient with fixed policy chain + positional injection
patterns.description: OpenMeterClient.__init__ builds AsyncPipelineClient from an ordered policy list (headers → user_agent → proxy → ContentDecodePolicy → retry → authentication → logging) and injects (self._client, self._config, self._serialize, self._deserialize) as positional args into every Operations class.

patterns.name: Async context manager protocol
patterns.description: OpenMeterClient implements __aenter__/__aexit__ and close() for `async with OpenMeterClient(...) as client`; __aenter__ delegates to self._client.__aenter__() and returns self.
patterns.example: async def __aenter__(self) -> Self: await self._client.__aenter__(); return self

patterns.name: send_request formats {endpoint} via deepcopy
patterns.description: send_request deep-copies the HttpRequest and formats the {endpoint} placeholder through self._client.format_url() before delegating — never pass a raw URL.

patterns.name: AsyncRetryPolicy in configuration
patterns.description: _configuration.py uses policies.AsyncRetryPolicy (not the sync RetryPolicy); authentication_policy defaults to None and must be supplied by callers via kwargs.

patterns.name: _patch.py customization hook
patterns.description: All hand-written customizations belong in _patch.py; __init__.py imports _patch_all and calls patch_sdk() after importing the generated client. Never edit _client.py directly.

key_file_guides.role: Defines OpenMeterClient: builds AsyncPipelineClient, owns the policy chain, instantiates every Operations attribute.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated — do not hand-edit. Duplicate attribute assignments (e.g. self.subjects twice) are a codegen artifact; new resource groups require SDK regeneration.

key_file_guides.role: OpenMeterClientConfiguration: stores endpoint, sdk_moniker, polling_interval, and all policy instances consumed by _client.py.
key_file_guides.watch_for: authentication_policy defaults to None (no auth) — callers must pass it via kwargs; use AsyncRetryPolicy here, not sync RetryPolicy.

key_file_guides.role: Empty hook for safe hand-written customizations; patch_sdk() runs at every import.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any logic added must be idempotent — it runs at every import; keep __all__ updated when adding public symbols.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point: re-exports OpenMeterClient and merges _patch_all into __all__.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The TYPE_CHECKING guard around `from ._patch import *` is intentional for linters — do not remove it.

key_file_guides.role: Async Operations classes; async def methods delegate request building to the sync build_* functions in the parent operations layer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated and overwritten on regeneration; per-method error_map handles typed deserialization — do not hand-edit.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing _client.py or operations/_operations.py — generated and overwritten on the next SDK regeneration.
Instantiating Operations classes directly rather than via OpenMeterClient attributes — bypasses shared serializer/deserializer injection.
Passing a bare URL to send_request without formatting the {endpoint} placeholder via self._client.format_url().
Raising on a streaming error response before `await response.read()` — leaks the socket/connection.
Adding business or retry logic inside _patch.py instead of the Operations layer.

decisions.decision: Async operations only handle execution and deserialization; all request-building lives in the sync operations/_operations.py build_* functions.
decisions.rationale: Avoids duplicating URL/param construction and keeps sync and async layers consistent from a single generated source.

decisions.decision: OpenMeterClientConfiguration is a separate class injected into every Operations constructor rather than raw kwargs.
decisions.rationale: Centralises policy instantiation (retry, auth, logging) and makes the whole policy chain replaceable at construction without subclassing the client.

code_examples.scenario: Async context manager usage with custom auth and a raw send_request call
code_examples.code:
from corehttp.runtime.pipeline import BearerTokenCredentialPolicy
from openmeter._generated.aio import OpenMeterClient
from corehttp.rest import HttpRequest

async def main():
    async with OpenMeterClient(
        endpoint="https://openmeter.cloud",
        authentication_policy=BearerTokenCredentialPolicy(credential, "api"),
    ) as client:
        meters = await client.meters.list()
        req = HttpRequest("GET", "{endpoint}/api/v1/meters")
        resp = await client.send_request(req)
